houseguests.bio: High-energy and expects to make close friendships in the house. Plans to bring fun, strong morals and 'chitter-chatter' to Season 28, while noting people sometimes underestimate her as 'ditzy' or 'air-heady.'
houseguests.name: Ashley Trail
houseguests.summary: Ashley missed her shot at premiere night safety and is now on the block ahead of the first eviction, after Mallory won the Week 1 veto and came off. With no comp wins and no alliance to her name, she enters the first vote in a genuinely dangerous spot.

houseguests.bio: Has watched BB since he was 8 and applied for years. Charming, spicy and social — strategy is becoming everyone's best friend. At 6'6" he's often underestimated and thinks the game could be a 'breeze.' Admits his mouth can get ahead of his brain; could become a villain by smiling in someone's face while planning to stab them in the back. Wears his emotions on his sleeve.
houseguests.name: Barrett Pfeiffer
houseguests.summary: Barrett missed out on competing for safety on premiere night yet landed a spot in the Crossovers alliance alongside Dee, Devens, Angela, and Drew. He narrowly avoided the block after Kamu lobbied Dee to nominate him, but Dee stuck to her plan and spared him for now.

houseguests.bio: Fun, easygoing and bubbly; plans a strong social game. A first-generation immigrant who believes he can talk to anyone. Strategy: win over the women first, build trust, make people feel like he's 'one of us.' People may assume he's conceited, but he's 'super chill.' Open to a showmance but would hate to be stuck in one. Handles conflict straight-up.
houseguests.name: Chuk Anyanwu
houseguests.summary: Chuk won premiere night safety, quickly bonded with Kamu and Haley, and joined Dee and Devens as a founding member of the Red Corner alliance. The downside: his group's poor HOH showing earned him a week as a Have-Not heading into the first eviction.

houseguests.bio: Outgoing, fun and unpredictable; watched BB since he was 8. Plans to make friends while knowing when to cut them. Viewers may see a 'beach bum without a brain,' but he graduated summa cum laude. Biggest strength: connecting with people; biggest weakness: his 'big fat mouth.' Expects to play from the shadows — and jokes the house may be rocking the 'Drewth' haircut by season's end.
houseguests.name: Drew Campbell
houseguests.summary: Drew is a member of the Crossovers alliance with Dee, Devens, Angela, and Barrett, giving him solid protection heading into the first eviction. The downside: his group's last-place HOH finish earned him a week as a Have-Not alongside Chuk, Haley, and Taylor.

houseguests.bio: Outgoing, bubbly and fun — but a new watcher, not a longtime fan. Believes she can win with strategy, intelligence and physical ability. Plan: listen, make friends fast, be everyone's friend early, then turn up the pressure. People may find her intimidating, but she's a bubbly non-stop talker. Biggest strength: her heart and handling conflict well.
houseguests.name: Haley Thogmartin
houseguests.summary: Haley is enduring a Have-Not week after her group finished last in the HOH comp, but she's already a founding member of the Red Corner alliance with Dee, Devens, Chuk, and Kamu. A rough start physically, but her social footing looks strong heading into the first vote.

houseguests.bio: Known by the stage name Salina EsTitties — appeared on RuPaul's Drag Race Season 15 and All Stars Season 11. A low-key BB superfan since Season 16. Ready to ruffle feathers and plans to take a page from Taylor Hale's playbook.
houseguests.name: Jason De Puy
houseguests.summary: Jason won his group's premiere night memory challenge, bringing Rachel Reilly into the house, but has stayed off the block and out of the alliance spotlight since. He heads into the first eviction quietly safe with a comp win already on his résumé.

houseguests.bio: Full name Kamuela 'Kamu' Kirk. Battle-tested from MMA — 11 or 12 surgeries from fighting. Plans a strong social game plus comp wins, but doesn't want to be pegged as a physical threat right away; may hide that he's fought at the sport's highest level and that his family owns his training facility. People may assume he's mean, but he cares about people. Possible weakness: too trusting.
houseguests.name: Kamu Kirk
houseguests.summary: Kamu is a founding member of the Red Corner alliance with Dee, Devens, Chuk, and Haley, but already flashed an independent streak by lobbying Dee to nominate Barrett instead of following her original plan — a push Dee ultimately rejected. A bold early move that could define how allies see him.

houseguests.bio: Goes by 'Lala' — 'fantabulistic fantastic,' watching BB since Season 2. Wants to become the oldest winner in BB history; social game plus physical play (she's competed in bodybuilding). Divorced mother of four; her weakness may be caring too much. Open to a showmance. Was part of Sounds of Blackness and is now tour coordinator for Kool & the Gang — Kool is her uncle.
houseguests.name: LaTrice Verrett
houseguests.summary: LaTrice competed in the premiere night balance challenge but didn't win safety, and has kept a low profile since, staying off the block and out of alliance conversations as the house heads into its first eviction vote.

houseguests.bio: Outgoing, positive and funny — often underestimated. Passed the bar at 23 (youngest in Hawaii, she says). Plans to be both a social player and comp threat; people see sweet and kind but don't realize she's athletic (gym twice a day, sings in a luau show). Open to a showmance if it doesn't hurt her game. Her father sang 'Nothing's Gonna Change My Love for You' in the '80s.
houseguests.name: Lyric Medeiros
houseguests.summary: Lyric has stayed off the block and out of the comp spotlight, but her growing flirtation with Rome has already become one of the most-noticed storylines in the house heading into the first eviction.

houseguests.bio: Fun, outgoing and energetic; will lean on social game, athleticism and confidence. People may assume she's an airhead — a major misconception. Plans to hide how smart she is, including not telling the full truth about her job. Has a side that gets a little villainous when push comes to shove — wants to crush enemies with 'logic.'
houseguests.name: Mallory Aurichio
houseguests.summary: Mallory was nominated as part of Dee's one-from-each-group strategy, but fought back by winning the Week 1 veto and pulling herself off the block. She survives the immediate danger and now has both a comp win and a narrow escape on her early résumé.

houseguests.bio: Ambitious, bold and funny — watched BB since she was 'in the womb.' Strategy: align with the right people and let others underestimate the 'dumb blonde sorority girl' look; she's intelligent and quick-witted. Won't reveal her real job — she'll say she's a waitress (also true). Not interested in a showmance; sensitivity is a weakness.
houseguests.name: Melody Morris
houseguests.summary: Melody competed in her group's premiere night challenge but didn't win safety, and has kept a quiet game since, staying off the block and out of alliance conversations as the first eviction approaches.

houseguests.bio: Survivor legend crossing over as a surprise premiere-night entrant — his first time playing Big Brother. Finished fourth on Survivor: Edge of Extinction (2019), falling in the fire-making challenge, then became the breakout star of Survivor 50: saved himself at back-to-back Tribal Councils and won a MrBeast coin flip that doubled the season's prize to $2 million, finishing seventh. A morning news anchor whose hometown declared an official 'Rick Devens Day' for his charity work with wheelchair sports and animal rescue.
houseguests.name: Rick Devens
houseguests.summary: Devens entered as a reality icon, quickly buried his Survivor rivalry with Dee, and co-founded both the Red Corner alliance with Dee, Kamu, Chuk, and Haley and the Crossovers with Dee, Angela, Barrett, and Drew. His web of connections makes him one of the best-positioned players heading into the first vote.

houseguests.bio: Real name Jack — friends call him Rome; his mom still calls him Jack. Bashful, extroverted, extremely excitable — wants to be seen as an alpha athlete, but he's also a nerd who collects trading cards. Goal: protect the 'betas and submissives' and be their warrior. Sees himself as a hero; others may see a villain because he talks trash. Extremely competitive and open to a showmance.
houseguests.name: Rome Seymour
houseguests.summary: Rome won his group's premiere night balance challenge for early safety, and his flirtation with Lyric has become the house's most-noticed developing connection. He heads into the first eviction comfortable and drama-adjacent.

houseguests.bio: Watched BB since she was a little girl; bringing realness, light and relatability. Strategy: the 'Big B Blowout' — a three-part team approach with floaters and two tethered teams moving votes and comps. Social game is her biggest strength; likable to the young ones, an 'old soul' to the older ones. Looks sweet and innocent, but she's a hero coming out of a villain era — and may need to go back in.
houseguests.name: Taylor Brown
houseguests.summary: Taylor is on the block this week after her group finished last in the HOH comp and became Have-Nots, making her one of Dee's three Week 1 nominees. With Mallory off the block after winning the veto, Taylor now faces Ashley and Yash as her co-nominees in a fight for survival.

houseguests.bio: Returning fan favorite from BB26 (2024), announced as a surprise entrant on premiere night. She won the very first HOH of her season, was nominated seven times and saved by veto three times on her way to a sixth-place finish, and landed in the top three for America's Favorite Player. Known for her fiery confrontations — including the infamous 'Crazy Eyes' blowup. A mother of four and grandmother of three who helps retirees find their forever homes; also ran The Amazing Race with her daughter Lexi.
houseguests.name: Angela Murray
houseguests.summary: Angela entered as the season's first returning icon, gave Dee valuable HOH advice after her win, and secured a spot in the Crossovers alliance alongside Dee, Devens, Barrett, and Drew. She's well-connected and comfortable heading into the first eviction.

houseguests.bio: Funny, smart and fresh — feels 'born for this.' A casual viewer, secretly a nerd with a hidden talent for mazes. May lie about being a financial analyst. Brushes off conflict aimed at him, but if conflict breaks out between others, he'll get involved and look for opportunity. Jokes his weakness is a hairdresser — a 5'4" brunette who cuts hair could make him throw his game away.
houseguests.name: Yash Patel
houseguests.summary: Yash competed in premiere night's memory challenge but came up short, and Dee placed him on the block as his group's representative. With Mallory having won the veto and come off, he now sits alongside Taylor and Ashley fighting for survival in the season's first eviction.

houseguests.bio: Winner of Survivor 45. Julie's time-travel twist pulled her into the BB28 house at the end of premiere night as the seventeenth houseguest, replacing Rachel Reilly. Undrafted — her points are tracked for the season but don't count toward any team.
houseguests.name: Dee Valladares
houseguests.summary: Dee entered late as Rachel Reilly's replacement, won the Week 1 HOH, and immediately built two overlapping alliances — the Red Corner with Devens, Kamu, Chuk, and Haley, and the Crossovers adding Angela, Barrett, and Drew. She rebuffed Kamu's push to spare Barrett and nominated Mallory, Taylor, and Yash, though Mallory's veto win shook up the block.

takenOffBlock.label: Taken Off Block by Someone Else
